results.title: Redreaming the dark
results.creator_credit: Erica Glynn, Charlotte Seymour, AFTRS and Romaine Moreton
results.brief_description:
During an electricity blackout in the family apartment, a young intellectually challenged Black woman has her life suddenly thrown into turmoil. Her grandmother soothes the young girl, teaching her not only to embrace the dark but also to love her dark self. Together the three women “redream” the dark.
Cast: Emily Walker, Leah Purcell, Irma Woods
results.creator_credit_for_label: Erica Glynn, Charlotte Seymour, AFTRS and Romaine Moreton

results.title: Range of experience
results.creator_credit: Maryella Hatfield, Anne Pratten, Lisa Duff, Marc Gillezeau and AFTRS
results.brief_description:
It is the future - a world where the ability to experience life’s basic emotions has been lost to humanity and Virtual Reality has replaced dreams. The company Range of Experience Productions Inc has invented the Dream Base Selector, a machine which enables users to re-live experiences once created naturally by the subconscious mind. But it soon becomes apparent that the technology is slightly faulty…
Cast: Soledad Cordeaux

results.title: Louise
results.creator_credit: Steven McGregor, Joe Weatherstone, AFTRS and Kelly Schilling
results.brief_description: A short documentary about a woman named Louise Mitchie who was born as a replacement child - a child conceived and born in order to help with the grief experienced by parents who had lost their first child in infancy.

results.title: Left lane ends
results.creator_credit: Sofya Gollan, Steve Pasvolsky, AFTRS and Alice Addison
results.brief_description:
Not paid sex, not Sleep-Eez, not working the late-night shift can help Danny sleep. Not until his ritual gets invaded by a girl.
Cast: Sam Lewis, Kate Beahan, Kris McQuade, Russell Cheek

results.title: The Lama of Greystanes
results.brief_description:
Zatul Rinzin Rinpoche is the 11th reincarnation of the lama (or spiritual leader) of the highest monastery in the world, Rongbuk, which lies at the foot of Mount Everest in western Tibet.
In 1959, when the Chinese invaded Tibet, Rinzin was forced to flee his homeland. Now 57, he lives in Greystanes in the outer western suburbs of Sydney with his wife and three children.
Rinzin is no longer a monk but he is still a lama - a responsibility he must hold his entire life. He now works as a security guard to support himself, his family and his monastery back in Tibet.
results.description:

Rinzin is no longer a monk but he is still a lama - a responsibility he must hold his entire life. He now works as a security guard to support himself, his family and his monastery back in Tibet.

results.title: Grey
results.creator_credit: Steven McGregor, Joe Weatherstone, AFTRS and Kelly Schilling
results.brief_description:
Aboriginal couple Annika and Randle have very different ideas on how to approach the Sydney Harbour Bridge reconciliation march between black and white Australians. Annika thinks that they should be up there on that bridge showing their support while Randle prefers to show his support on a smaller, more personal scale.
After the march a white woman collapses and, while Randle is off calling for an ambulance, Annika stays with the woman. It is here that she is forced to confront her own prejudice and ultimately acknowledge that she just may be one of the biggest hypocrites of all.
Cast: Janine Matthews, Laurence Clifford, Victoria Longley
results.creator_credit_for_label: Steven McGregor, Joe Weatherstone, AFTRS and Kelly Schilling

results.title: Goblin market
results.creator_credit: Belinda Hall, Megan Harding, Rachel Perkins and AFTRS
results.brief_description:
“Morning and evening
maids heard the goblin cry,
come buy our orchid fruits
come buy, come buy”
Goblin market by Christina Rossetti
Adapted from the poem by Christina Rosette about an age-old myth as seen through contemporary Australian eyes.
Cast: Rachel Maza (Laura), Lisa Maza (Lizzie)

results.title: Glass
results.creator_credit: Safina Uberoi, Jacqui North and AFTRS
results.brief_description:
He is an obessive bookmaker. She is addicted to talk-back radio. A film about love, loneliness and the dark magic of dreams.
Cast: Odile Le Clezio, Lech Mackiewicz, Dean Vassallo

results.title: Fuse
results.creator_credit: Maia Horniak, Katherine Shortland, AFTRS and Marcus Waters
results.brief_description:
A young Aboriginal girl wants to revenge the death of her older brother who has died while in jail for a petty crime. He was the only person she had in the world and now, desperately alone and angry, she finds the judge that sentenced her brother.
The film unfolds in the judge’s bachelor apartment where he is tied up and held captive as each of them confront their own judgements and their own pain.
An intense drama between two completely different people in one room over one night unfolds as they both behave in unexpected ways. Human emotions cross between the race, sex and class divide as each rediscover the meaning of loneliness, loss and judgement.
Cast: Shai Pittman, Chris Haywood
results.creator_credit_for_label: Maia Horniak, Katherine Shortland, AFTRS and Marcus Waters

results.title: Figures in motion
results.creator_credit: Damien Power, Priscilla Collins, AFTRS and Glenn Weller
results.brief_description:
Eadweard Muybridge was the pioneer photographer whose experiments in stop-motion photography helped develop the technology that resulted in motion pictures. In this elegant fictional film, Muybridge forces his young wife to pose nude with his male lover. As the photographer begins to take his shots, he becomes more and more excited by the control he can exercise over both his lovers. An initially camp and light treatment of the erotics that forms the basis of voyeuristic desire in cinema, the film becomes progressively darker as Muybridge’s wife becomes the object of not only desire, but of appropriation and violence, signifying that even at the birth of cinema the male gaze was rapacious and pornographic.
Cast: Michael Percival, Lenore Munro, Anthony Cogin.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Damien Power, Priscilla Collins, AFTRS and Glenn Weller
